message:
feat(session): async coalescing index update dispatcher with retry

Replace the previous pattern of spawning ad-hoc threads and calling
SessionIndex::index_session_snapshot synchronously on every session persist
with a centralized, long-lived dispatcher thread that coalesces and retries
index updates.

Why: Under heavy multi-agent concurrent workloads, the old approach spawned
a new thread per save and could race on the SQLite index file. The new
dispatcher (pi-session-index-updater thread) batches updates via an mpsc
channel, coalesces duplicate updates to the same (sessions_root, path) key
so only the latest snapshot wins, and retries transient failures with
exponential backoff (up to 3 retries). This eliminates thread proliferation,
reduces SQLite lock contention, and makes index updates more resilient to
transient I/O errors.

Key changes:
- src/session.rs: All three persist paths (full-rewrite, append, and the
  fallback branch) now call enqueue_session_index_snapshot_update() instead
  of inline SessionIndex calls or thread::spawn wrappers. Added defensive
  guard for persisted_entry_count > entries.len() to force full rewrite
  when counts are out of sync.
- src/session_index.rs: New PendingIndexUpdate struct, IndexUpdateCommand
  enum, IndexUpdateDispatcher with OnceLock singleton, coalescing HashMap,
  retry_delay() with exponential backoff, process_pending_index_updates()
  flush logic, and flush_pending_updates() for synchronous drain in tests.
  Two new tests: async_index_updates_coalesce_latest_snapshot verifies that
  rapid-fire updates to the same session collapse to the latest values, and
  async_index_updates_retry_after_transient_failure verifies retry succeeds
  once the underlying file becomes available.
- src/session_test.rs: New test file for session save persistence.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
